npcs.name: Merchant Ishiyama
dialogue: I have wares if you dont have gold. haha
Prices are supposed to shift with alignment, but today I am testing suspiciously cheap mercy.

dialogue.text: Hello, what is your name?
Mysterious. I will call you Potato Stranger.
Howlu? Then the potatoes chose a very brave name.
Do you want me to send you to the forest path?

choices.message: The villager points you toward the trees.

npcs.name: Mayor Tuberlin
dialogue: Potato Haven is peaceful only because everyone is too scared to say what they want.

dialogue.text: If you speak for a faction, speak softly. If you speak for yourself, speak clearly.

quest.name: Keep Potato Haven's Peace
quest.description: Mayor Tuberlin wants Potato Haven to survive the growing faction pressure without becoming a battlefield. Stay balanced, listen to each side, and watch for signs that the town is changing around your choices.

dialogue.text: Then you may become the rarest thing here: a person all three factions hesitate to erase.
That is how every war starts sounding reasonable.

npcs.name: Mira Sprout
dialogue: My field grew the same potato twice yesterday. Same shape. Different memory.

dialogue.text: The Spud Wardens call it a wound in the Healing Map. Will you help restore damaged land?

quest.name: The Healing Map
quest.description: Mira Sprout believes the world can be healed by restoring fractured zones, purifying corrupted hubs, and stabilizing major regions before the Armada restructures them.

dialogue.text: Then follow living potato magic. It heals by listening first.
Maybe restoration means learning what the new world is trying to become.

npcs.name: Dock Engineer Brine
dialogue: A bridge that chooses its own destination is not magical. It is a liability.

dialogue.text: Boat Mom's Structure Protocol can secure unstable regions before they swallow towns like this one.

quest.name: The Structure Protocol
quest.description: Dock Engineer Brine wants you to secure unstable regions, deploy control nodes, suppress anomalies, and prove whether full control or hybrid order can keep the world alive.

dialogue.text: Good. Mercy without structure is just panic with softer words.
People are exactly what systems are built to protect. Usually.

npcs.name: Archivist Luma
dialogue: Hello again. Or first. I have notes supporting both.

dialogue.text: The Unwritten Truth begins with memory fragments. Will you preserve contradiction instead of solving it?

quest.name: The Unwritten Truth
quest.description: Archivist Luma asks you to collect memory fragments, observe faction conflicts, record paradox events, and decide whether many truths can survive together.

choices.text: One stable truth may be kinder

dialogue.text: Excellent. If reality repeats itself, take notes before it corrects you.
Kindness often edits the footnotes first.

npcs.name: Captain Saltfork
dialogue: The ocean fractured after the Starch Engine Incident. Some waves arrive before they leave.

dialogue.text: If you find the shore paths, watch the water. It remembers Boat Mom before the Armada did.

quest.name: Map the Ocean Fractures
quest.description: Captain Saltfork knows the sea routes broke when the Mopo fell into the world. Find shoreline anomalies and learn what the waves remember about Boat Mom.

dialogue.text: Good. A map is not control if you let the water disagree with you.
Spoken like a dock that has never met a storm.

npcs.name: Kid Spud
dialogue: I saw you tomorrow! You were taller. Or maybe the moon was closer.

dialogue.text: If all three groups like you the same amount, the Root Keeper stops naming your path.

quest.name: A Path Without a Name
quest.description: Kid Spud hints at a secret neutral path. Keep faction influence balanced, make contradictory decisions, and see whether the Root Keeper refuses to classify you.

dialogue.text: Neat. I will remember this before you do.
Clear sides are easier to draw and easier to cut out.

npcs.name: Guard Unit 07
dialogue: Unregistered variable detected. Please become predictable.

dialogue.text: Compliance reduces accidents. Resistance creates paperwork and sometimes craters.
Predictability accepted. Uncertainty reduced by a number I am not allowed to show you.
Refusal recorded. Please enjoy your temporary freedom responsibly.
Memory file unavailable. Memory file unavailable. Memory file singing quietly.

npcs.name: Old Farmer Crumb
dialogue: I remember the Golden Mash Age. I also remember it never happened. Both memories have dirt under their nails.

dialogue.text: Do not let anyone tell you contradiction is useless. Sometimes it is the only proof the world survived.

quest.name: Record Golden Mash Memories
quest.description: Old Farmer Crumb carries conflicting memories of the Golden Mash Age. Preserve his contradictions before the Root Archive or the Armada turns them into one clean story.

dialogue.text: Good. Write this down: the moon used to smell like rain and fries.
Also good. Memories do not grow dinner unless someone tends the dirt.
The traveler blinks once. It may have been a thank you.
The traveler touches the sprout like it recognizes the future.
The traveler shows you an empty hand. Somehow it feels official.

npcs.name: Spud Warden Mender
dialogue: The Mopo grass remembers the Golden Mash Age. It is weak, but still alive.

dialogue.text: Will you help us reconnect the meadow paths instead of forcing them into order?

quest.name: Restore the Mopo Paths
quest.description: Reconnect the unstable paths through Mopo Meadow and prove that living potato magic can be stabilized without being chained by recipe logic.

choices.text: I only want to record what happens

dialogue.text: Then listen for living potato magic. It bends, but it should not be chained.
That is Boat Mom's answer. It may save a road while killing the meadow.
A record can help, if you remember that roots are alive before they are evidence.

npcs.name: Armada Quartermaster
dialogue: Unregulated paths collapse. Unregulated people panic. Boat Mom understands this.

dialogue.text: Will you carry an Armada routing mark and help us impose safe structure?

quest.name: Carry the Armada Routing Mark
quest.description: Carry an Armada routing mark through unstable territory and test whether structure can protect the world without turning every path into an order.

choices.text: I will not help control the world

dialogue.text: Good. Structure is mercy when the world is splitting.
Then enjoy uncertainty. It eats roads first, then names.
Proof is a luxury, but perhaps your caution has tactical value.

npcs.name: Archivist Scribe
dialogue: Every version of the Starch Engine Incident is true somewhere. We preserve the contradictions.

dialogue.text: What should be done with conflicting memories?

quest.name: Catalog Conflicting Truths
quest.description: Preserve conflicting memories inside the Archivist Vault and learn why contradiction might be evidence rather than corruption.

choices.text: Heal the one world we still have
Choose the version that can be controlled

dialogue.text: Then you understand us. Contradiction is not always corruption.
A kind answer. Dangerous, but kind.
Control creates clean records. It also deletes the messy ones.

npcs.name: Root Keeper
dialogue: You have returned to Root Home.
I remember every time you fall.
The Mopo does not forget. Neither, now, do the factions.

dialogue.text: Where should the roots carry you?
Then rest for a moment. The Root Home is quiet.

npcs.name: Root Keeper Echo
dialogue: I am not the Root Keeper. I am the version that remembers what he forgets.

dialogue.text: The Root Archive wants one consistent truth. That would make the world easier to explain and smaller to live in.

quest.name: Protect Alternate Outcomes
quest.description: The Root Keeper Echo warns that the final Root Archive could merge all memories into one interpretation, erasing alternate outcomes and player freedom. Preserve contradiction until the final choice appears.

choices.text: A single truth may save reality

dialogue.text: Then when the archive asks you to become simple, remember how hard you fought to stay whole.
A single truth is sharp. Hold it carefully, or it will cut away more than lies.
